heading: Regional children’s advocacy centers
label: 20303 Regional children’s advocacy centers
notes: References in TextThis Act, referred to in subsec. (c)(6), probably means the Victims of Child Abuse Act of 1990, title II of , , , which is classified principally to this chapter. For complete classification of title II to the Code, see Short Title of 1990 Act note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 101–647104 Stat. 4792section 10101 of this titleNov. 29, 1990
CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 13001b of Title 42
Prior ProvisionsA prior was renumbered section 214A and is classified to .section 213 of Pub. L. 101–647section 20305 of this title

Termination of Advisory BoardsAdvisory boards established after , to terminate not later than the expiration of the 2-year period beginning on the date of their establishment, unless, in the case of a board established by the President or an officer of the Federal Government, such board is renewed by appropriate action prior to the expiration of such 2-year period, or in the case of a board established by Congress, its duration is otherwise provided by law. See sections 1001(2) and 1013 of Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.Jan. 5, 1973

children.heading: Establishment and maintenance of regional children’s advocacy program
children.label: a Establishment and maintenance of regional children’s advocacy program

children.text: focus attention on child victims by assisting communities in developing child-focused, community-oriented, facility-based programs designed to improve the resources available to children and families;
enhance coordination among community agencies and professionals involved in the intervention, prevention, prosecution, and investigation systems that respond to child abuse cases;

train physicians and other health care and mental health care professionals, law enforcement officers, child protective service workers, forensic interviewers, prosecutors, victim advocates, multidisciplinary team leadership, and children’s advocacy center staff, in the multidisciplinary approach to child abuse so that trained personnel will be available to provide support to community agencies and professionals involved in the intervention, prevention, prosecution, and investigation systems that respond to child abuse cases;
provide technical assistance, training, coordination, and organizational capacity support for State chapters; and

children.label: A
children.text: local children’s advocacy centers; and

children.label: B
children.text: communities that want to develop local children’s advocacy centers.

children.heading: Activities of regional children’s advocacy program
children.label: b Activities of regional children’s advocacy program
A
children.text: establish and maintain regional children’s advocacy program centers; and

children.label: B
children.text: fund existing regional centers with expertise in multidisciplinary team investigation, trauma-informed interventions, and evidence-informed treatment,

children.label: i
children.text: in developing and expanding a comprehensive, multidisciplinary response to child abuse that is designed to meet the needs of child victims and their families;

children.label: I
children.text: organizational support and development;

children.label: II
children.text: programmatic evaluation; and

children.label: III
children.text: financial oversight of Federal funding;

children.label: iii
children.text: in establishing child-friendly facilities for the investigation and intervention in child abuse;

children.label: iv
children.text: in preventing or reducing trauma to children caused by duplicative contacts with community professionals;

children.label: v
children.text: in providing families with needed services and assisting them in regaining maximum functioning;

children.label: vi
children.text: in maintaining open communication and case coordination among community professionals and agencies involved in child protection efforts;

children.label: vii
children.text: in coordinating and tracking investigative, preventive, prosecutorial, and treatment efforts;

children.label: viii
children.text: in obtaining information useful for criminal and civil proceedings;

children.label: ix
children.text: in holding offenders accountable through improved prosecution of child abuse cases;

children.label: x
children.text: in enhancing professional skills necessary to effectively respond to cases of child abuse through training; and

children.label: xi
children.text: in enhancing community understanding of child abuse; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide training and technical assistance to local children’s advocacy centers, interested communities, and chapters in its census region that are grant recipients under .section 20304 of this title

children.heading: Operation of regional children’s advocacy program
children.label: c Operation of regional children’s advocacy program

children.heading: Solicitation of proposals
children.label: 1 Solicitation of proposals
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Administrator shall solicit proposals for assistance under this section.November 4, 1992

children.label: A
children.text: A proven record in conducting activities of the kinds described in subsection (c).

children.label: B
children.text: A facility where children who are victims of sexual or physical abuse and their nonoffending family members can go for the purpose of investigation and intervention in child abuse.

children.label: C
children.text: Multidisciplinary staff experienced in providing evidence-informed services for children and families.

children.label: D
children.text: Experience in serving as a center for training and education and as a resource facility.

children.label: E
children.text: National expertise in providing technical assistance to communities with respect to the multidisciplinary response to child abuse.

children.label: i
children.text: include a single or multiyear management plan that outlines how the applicant will provide information, services, and technical assistance to communities and chapters so that communities can establish and maintain multidisciplinary programs that respond to child abuse and chapters can establish and maintain children’s advocacy centers in their State;

children.label: ii
children.text: demonstrate the ability of the applicant to operate successfully a children’s advocacy center or provide training to allow others to do so; and

children.label: iii
children.text: state the annual cost of the proposal and a breakdown of those costs.

children.label: i
children.text: outline the basic activities expected to be performed;

children.label: ii
children.text: describe the entities that will conduct the basic activities;

children.label: iii
children.text: establish the period of time over which the basic activities will take place; and

children.label: I
children.text: identifying managerial, organizational, and administrative procedures and responsibilities;

children.label: II
children.text: demonstrating how implementation and monitoring of the progress of the children’s advocacy program after receipt of funding will be achieved; and

children.label: III
children.text: providing sufficient rationale to support the costs of the plan.

children.heading: Competitive basis
children.label: A Competitive basis
children.text: Proposals shall be selected under this section on a competitive basis.

children.label: i
children.text: best result in developing and establishing multidisciplinary programs that respond to child abuse by assisting, training, and teaching community agencies and professionals called upon to respond to child abuse cases;

children.label: ii
children.text: assist in resolving problems that may occur during the development, operation, and implementation of a multidisciplinary program that responds to child abuse;

children.label: iii
children.text: to the greatest extent possible and subject to available appropriations, ensure that at least 1 applicant is selected from each of the 4 census regions of the country;

children.label: iv
children.text: best result in supporting chapters in each State; and

children.label: v
children.text: otherwise best carry out the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Funding of program
children.label: 5 Funding of program
children.text: From amounts made available in separate appropriation Acts, the Administrator shall provide to each grant recipient the financial and technical assistance and other incentives that are necessary and appropriate to carry out this section.

children.heading: Coordination of effort
children.label: 6 Coordination of effort
children.text: In order to carry out activities that are in the best interests of abused and neglected children, a grant recipient shall consult with other grant recipients under this Act on a regular basis to exchange ideas, share information, and review children’s advocacy program activities.

children.heading: Evaluation of regional children’s advocacy program activities
children.label: 1 Evaluation of regional children’s advocacy program activities
children.text: The Administrator shall regularly monitor and evaluate the activities of grant recipients and shall determine whether each grant recipient has complied with the original proposal and any modifications.

children.label: A
children.text: describes the progress made in satisfying the purpose of the children’s advocacy program; and

children.label: B
children.text: states whether changes are needed and are being made to carry out the purpose of the children’s advocacy program.

children.heading: Discontinuation of funding
children.label: 3 Discontinuation of funding
children.text: Upon discontinuation of funding of a grant recipient under this section, the Administrator shall solicit new proposals in accordance with subsection (c).
